items.title: Mine may be shut down amid money laundering allegations, bikie claims

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

gallery.altText: An aerial of mining buildings in a forest and huge mountains in the background.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial of mining buildings in a forest and huge mountains in the background.

avatar.alt: A man with light green glasses smiles.

items.title: Report card issued on Antarctic division's cultural reforms

children.content: Workplace reforms implemented by the Australian Antarctic Division (AAD) after two damning reports into the organisation's culture have led to some improvements but a review has found problems persist.

image.altText: Casey Station photographed from above.

items.title: Safety officers to patrol Hobart CBD to curb antisocial behaviour

children.content: A trio of council-employed public safety monitors will soon be patrolling "hot spots" in the Hobart CBD, to try to curb antisocial behaviour and theft.

image.altText: An empty shopping mall strip.

items.title: Calls for tourism lease of world heritage area island to be cancelled

children.content: A private developer who wants to build a standing camp on an island in the Tasmanian Wilderness World Heritage Area tells the ABC he has requested another lease extension, amid an outcry from the Tasmanian Greens.

gallery.altText: A picturesque lake on a cloudy day. The far horizon is slightly obscured by fog, but trees are visible closer to the forefront
a rustic hut in Australian scrub

thumbnail.altText: A picturesque lake on a cloudy day. The far horizon is slightly obscured by fog, but trees are visible closer to the forefront
a rustic hut in Australian scrub

items.title: Regional Australians more likely to die from MND, according to new study

children.content: Tasmanian grandmother Kelli Skipper had to travel for hours to get the medical treatment she needed after being diagnosed with a form of motor neurone disease. She is now calling for change.

image.altText: A woman and a young child sitting on a chair smiling at the camera.

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: ASX jumps on back of massive Wall St rally amid war end hopes

children.content: Wall Street ends a weak month with a massive gain, as investors eye a possible end to US military action in Iran, and Australian shares follow. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: 'It's tragic': Hundreds to be impacted after Seafood giant folds

children.content: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

image.altText: raptis fleets docked at the karumba wharf

items.title: Freeman joins a long line of fugitives captured or killed in Australia

children.content: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

articles.title: Dezi Freeman died isolated from the modern world, but not far from it

children.content: The fugitive resurfaced more than 150 kilometres away from the town where he shot and killed two police officers seven months ago. Mystified locals are questioning how.

image.altText: A road leading through Thologolong, with a blue and white sign beside the tree-lined road.

articles.title: 'Evil inside him': Dezi Freeman's nephew speaks out on cop killer uncle's death

children.content: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead, it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

image.altText: Two men smiling for a photo in a backyard.

gallery.altText: A photo of Dezi and an image of Ned Kelly

thumbnail.altText: A photo of Dezi and an image of Ned Kelly

items.title: House prices rise everywhere besides Sydney and Melbourne

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

image.altText: A man and a woman smile at the camera.

items.title: Anger at developer's plan to build $10m mansion on illegally cleared land

children.content: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

image.altText: A render of a house.

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

articles.title: Italy fails to reach third straight World Cup after penalty shootout loss

children.content: Italy's World Cup exile continues after the four-time champions fall to a 4-1 penalty shootout defeat by Bosnia and Herzegovina.

image.altText: Pio Esposito reacts after missing a penalty against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

articles.title: Socceroos thump Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell, as Irankunda electrifies

children.content: Nestory Irankunda scores twice as the Socceroos' substitutes spark a 5-1 win over Curaçao in their FIFA Series clash in Melbourne.

image.altText: Irankunda backflip

items.title: Star completes sale of Queen's Wharf stake to Hong Kong partners

children.content: Star Entertainment confirms it has relinquished its stake in the Destination Brisbane Consortium to Hong Kong partners Chow Tai Fook Enterprises and Far East Consortium.

image.altText: A sign that reads "The Star" below a star shape on the side of a high rise building.

items.title: NFL accused of being 'sexist institution' by first female official

children.content: One of the first female officials to be hired by the NFL, Robin DeLorenzo, says that she suffered "open hostility" and "gender-based scrutiny" during her three years as an official in the league.

image.altText: Robin DeLorenzo looks up

items.title: This controversial rom-com starring Zendaya is one of the best movies of the year

children.content: On the surface, A-listers Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new film seems like a regular rom-com. That's exactly what director Kristoffer Borgli wants you to think.

items.title: Travel quiz: Get out your passport and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which is Australia’s oldest Luna Park? And where would you find Elvis Presley’s mansion?

image.altText: Sydney Luna Park entrance

items.title: Bluey's lead composer on helping kids fall in love with music

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: A man in a colourful shirt plays the melodica

items.title: Judge halts Trump's White House demolition and ballroom project

children.content: A federal court has ordered the Trump administration to suspend work on a $400 million ballroom on the site of the demolished East Wing of the White House, barring construction from proceeding without congressional approval.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump holds up a picture of a grand ballroom while he sits in the Oval Office

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump holds up a picture of a grand ballroom while he sits in the Oval Office

items.title: Childhood vaccine coverage declines for fifth year in a row

children.content: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

image.altText: A nurse holds a needle up to a young boy's arm

items.title: Police allege murder victim's body was set alight after death

children.content: A 37-year-old man has been arrested and charged with murder a week after burning human remains were found at a Dimbulah property.

image.altText: A police officer standing next to a trailer in a backyard.

items.title: Phone ban in classrooms extended to include smartwatches, headphones

children.content: Victorian students in public and private schools will be banned from wearing smartwatches with internet connectivity and using headphones in classrooms from next year after the government announced an extension of the device ban in schools.

image.altText: Close up of a smart watch, as worn by a seven-year-old boy.

items.title: Seafood industry pleads for public to buy local for Easter as fuel costs bite

children.content: Seafood operators are struggling to stay afloat, with the nation's biggest wild-caught tuna and swordfish company confirming it is shutting up shop.

image.altText: Man smiling with prawns

items.title: Man jailed for stabbing wife to death eligible for parole in two weeks

children.content: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: Regional residents call for fairer share of WA's public transport services

children.content: Despite living in a city of more than 40,000 people, 85-year-old Jan Whalan faces an hour's trip on her mobility scooter to get to the shops.

image.altText: Portrait shot of Yalyalup resident Jan Whalan riding her mobility scooter.

items.title: Farm accident leaves teen paralysed but not ready to quit the land

children.content: A Queensland teenager paralysed after a hay bale accident on his family farm is determined to return to the land despite the challenges of life in a wheelchair.

gallery.altText: A younger man in a wheelchair with horses behind him.
A man in a wheelchair at the center of the image with an older woman and older man on either side of him.
A man in a wheelchair on the football field with a team of players.

thumbnail.altText: A younger man in a wheelchair with horses behind him.
A man in a wheelchair at the center of the image with an older woman and older man on either side of him.
A man in a wheelchair on the football field with a team of players.

items.title: Less than half of states signed up as national gun reform deadline passes

children.content: The federal government's March deadline for national gun reform has come and gone, with more than half the states and territories still opposed to parts of the plan. Only four have committed to a gun buyback scheme.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wearing glasses and looking stern.

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.

items.title: Farmers think outside the box as rising fuel costs add to drought woes

children.content: Farmers are turning to roadside grazing, selling up stock, and, in one case, shipping cattle to an island to navigate the drought and the rising cost of diesel.

image.altText: A ute drives along a road where black cattle graze either side.

items.title: The list that helps Aussie surfing star handle the noise in her head

children.content: Reigning world surfing champion Molly Picklum kicks her off title defence this week at Bells Beach, where writing a list may form part of her preparation.

image.altText: Molly Picklum paddles through the ocean on a surfboard.

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

gallery.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

thumbnail.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

items.title: Hundreds of thousands in flood damages leave tourism vendors devastated

children.content: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

image.altText: A kitchen full of floodwater.

items.title: Shein and Kmart accused of copying Queensland retailer's designs

children.content: Queensland business Sabo Skirt has claimed Shein, Kmart and numerous other retailers and wholesalers have copied its clothing patterns, prints and trademarks on "lower quality" garments in a federal court claim.

image.altText: A graphic with a dress with an aquatic print and a Sabo logo next to Shein and Kmart logos over aquatic print garments.

items.title: Why your household could be hit by a bill hike trifecta this month

children.content: Millions of Australian households will be hit with a trifecta of price hikes this month. Here's what to know about the changes and how they could affect you.

image.altText: Cash money in a purse with a calculator, tea towel, list of bills on a notepad and a pen

items.title: Woods to seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI

children.content: Tiger Woods says he will step away and seek treatment, days after he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

image.altText: Golfer Tiger Woods stands by his overturned vehicle

items.title: Cocoa prices crash but Easter chocolate stays expensive

children.content: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

gallery.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

thumbnail.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.
